passage.title: Word Card Study Test
passage.text: Our reading club tested two ways to remember new words last month. Sora used one big notebook, but Theo used small word cards. After one week, Theo remembered more words because he could review the cards many times. The club counted each correct answer and wrote the totals in a notebook. When Sora saw the difference, she changed to small word cards. Three days later, she also answered the review game more quickly. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that short review tools can help students study a little at a time.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Learning a better way to remember new words
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Theo use to study?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: The way two things are not the same
A loud sound in a room
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does review mean in the third sentence?
choices: A person who visits a club
Look at something again to remember it
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Sora switch to small word cards?
choices: The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
She thought shorter practice would help her remember more
The teacher told the student to stop working
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
